title: qa_execution_failed event payload
$comment: Provenance: authored 2026-04-23 in WU 3.3 as the QA execution failure signal. Pairs with qa_execution_completed.schema.json. Idempotence key is (task_correlation_id, commit_sha): the qa-execution skill refuses to emit a second event with the same pair. Downstream consumer WU 3.4 (qa-regression) keys its first-failure vs. repeat-failure decision on this event plus the existence of prior regression artifacts for the same (implementation_task_correlation_id, test_id) pair; the implementation_task_correlation_id is resolved by WU 3.4 from the feature task graph (SOT = feature registry), NOT carried in this payload, per WU 3.3 §'Decisions' D3.
description: Payload shape for the `qa_execution_failed` event emitted by the QA agent on any qa-execution run where one or more tests failed. The event is the machine-readable signal downstream regression handling keys off; the original implementation task's state is untouched (cross-task regression invariant — see agents/qa/CLAUDE.md §'Cross-task regression semantics').

task_correlation_id.description: Task-level correlation ID of the qa_execution task that produced this event. Together with `commit_sha`, forms the idempotence key.

commit_sha.description: Git commit SHA of the assigned repo's `main` HEAD at execution time. 40-char full SHA; short SHAs are rejected.

plan_path.description: Path to the test plan file that was executed, relative to the product specs repo root.

test_count.description: Number of tests in the plan's `tests` array at execution time. The pass count is `test_count - len(failed_tests)` and is derivable, not duplicated.

failed_tests.description: One entry per test that did not pass. Order follows the order tests appear in the plan file (stable, not evidence-severity-sorted).

test_id.description: The `test_id` of the failing test, matching the `test_id` field in the plan file's `tests[]` entry. Stability is load-bearing here: qa-regression (WU 3.4) keys regression artifacts on `(implementation_task_correlation_id, test_id)`, and the test_id match is how the repeat-failure path distinguishes new failures from known-open regressions.

first_signal.description: Short human-readable excerpt that substantiates the failure — the most load-bearing line from stdout, stderr, or the exit-code summary. Long outputs must be truncated to an excerpt here; the full output may be referenced from the verification trail but is not inlined. Secrets must be redacted per `shared/rules/security-boundaries.md` §'Log hygiene'.

exit_code.description: Optional exit code of the last command run for this test, when the failure is signalled by a non-zero exit. Omitted when the failure is detected by predicate mismatch against a command that returned `0`.

stderr_excerpt.description: Optional excerpt of stderr from the failing command, when stderr carries signal distinct from stdout. Same redaction discipline as `first_signal`.

total_duration_seconds.description: Wall-clock seconds for the full per-test loop. Optional on failures: a run that crashed partway may legitimately lack a duration.
